# Ch. 7: Schedule maintenance


Some work isn't triggered by a request; it runs on a clock. In this chapter you give links an
optional expiry, then add a `link-sweeper` worker that deletes the expired ones on a schedule with
`iii-cron`.

## Add the workers

`iii-cron` fires functions on a schedule. The `link-sweeper` worker is yours, so scaffold it the
same way you scaffolded `link` in Chapter 1:

```bash
iii worker add iii-cron
iii worker init link-sweeper --language typescript
```

## Give links an expiry

A sweep needs something to sweep. Add an optional `expires_at` column to the `links` table in the
`link` worker's `ensureSchema`:

```typescript link/src/index.ts {5}
await worker.trigger({
  function_id: "database::execute",
  payload: {
    db: DB,
    sql: "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS links (code TEXT PRIMARY KEY, url TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TEXT NOT NULL, expires_at TEXT)",
  },
});
```

Accept `expires_at` in `link::create` and store it (it stays `null` for links that never expire):

```typescript link/src/index.ts {3,9,10}
worker.registerFunction(
  "link::create",
  async (payload: { url: string; code?: string; expires_at?: string }) => {
    const code = payload.code ?? makeCode();
    await worker.trigger({
      function_id: "database::execute",
      payload: {
        db: DB,
        sql: "INSERT INTO links (code, url, created_at, expires_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
        params: [code, payload.url, new Date().toISOString(), payload.expires_at ?? null],
      },
    });
    await worker.trigger({
      function_id: "state::set",
      payload: { scope: "links", key: code, value: { url: payload.url } },
    });
    await worker.trigger({
      function_id: "publish",
      payload: { topic: "link.created", data: { code, url: payload.url } },
    });
    logger.info("link created", { code, url: payload.url });
    return { code, url: payload.url };
  },
);
```

## Sweep expired links on a schedule

The `link-sweeper` worker owns the cleanup. It queries the database for expired links, evicts them
from the `iii-state` cache, deletes the rows, and binds to a `cron` trigger. The cron expression has
**seven** fields, starting with seconds and ending with year (`0 0 3 * * * *` is 03:00 every day,
any year). Replace the generated `link-sweeper/src/index.ts`:

```typescript link-sweeper/src/index.ts
import { registerWorker, Logger } from "iii-sdk";

const worker = registerWorker(process.env.III_URL ?? "ws://localhost:49134", {
  workerName: "link-sweeper",
});
const logger = new Logger();
const DB = "primary";

worker.registerFunction("link-sweeper::sweep_expired", async () => {
  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const { rows } = await worker.trigger<
    { db: string; sql: string; params: string[] },
    { rows: Array<{ code: string }> }
  >({
    function_id: "database::query",
    payload: {
      db: DB,
      sql: "SELECT code FROM links WHERE expires_at IS NOT NULL AND expires_at < ?",
      params: [now],
    },
  });
  for (const { code } of rows) {
    await worker.trigger({ function_id: "state::delete", payload: { scope: "links", key: code } });
  }
  await worker.trigger({
    function_id: "database::execute",
    payload: {
      db: DB,
      sql: "DELETE FROM links WHERE expires_at IS NOT NULL AND expires_at < ?",
      params: [now],
    },
  });
  logger.info("swept expired links", { count: rows.length });
  return { swept: rows.length };
});

worker.registerTrigger({
  type: "cron",
  function_id: "link-sweeper::sweep_expired",
  // Production: 03:00 every day. For the tutorial, sweep every 15 seconds so you can watch it fire.
  config: { expression: "*/15 * * * * * *" },
});

logger.info("link-sweeper ready");
```

Register it with your project:

```bash
iii worker add ./link-sweeper
```

`sweep_expired` is an ordinary function, so you can run it on demand to test rather than waiting for
the schedule to fire.

## See it work

Create one link that already expired and one that doesn't, then run the sweep by hand:

```bash
iii trigger link::create url=https://old.example code=old expires_at=2000-01-01T00:00:00Z
iii trigger link::create url=https://keep.example code=keep
iii trigger link-sweeper::sweep_expired
```

```json
{ "swept": 1 }
```

The expired link is gone from both the database and the cache, while the other still resolves:

```bash
iii trigger link::resolve code=old
iii trigger link::resolve code=keep
```

```json
{ "url": null }
{ "url": "https://keep.example" }
```

## Conclusion

Linkly now maintains itself: a dedicated `link-sweeper` worker expires stale links on a schedule,
with no request needed to trigger it. Next, in
[Ch. 8: Bring in the browser](/tutorials/linkly/frontend), you turn a browser tab into a worker that
creates links and subscribes to the live click stream.
